# Investigation: Revenue Drivers by Product Category

## Objective
- Analyze revenue distribution across product categories
- Identify external market benchmarks for comparison

## Approach
Decomposed revenue analysis into per-category breakdowns, then validated against published industry reports using WebSearch.

## Key Findings
- Electronics accounts for 68% of total revenue, consistent with industry avg of 60-70% for mixed retail [source: https://example.com/retail-benchmarks-2025]
- North region Electronics drives 42% of category revenue [source: https://example.com/regional-sales-report]
- Discount elasticity in Clothing is -1.3, meaning 10% discount yields 13% volume increase [source: https://example.com/pricing-elasticity-study]
- Food category shows stable but low margins at 8.2% vs industry avg of 7-10% [source: https://example.com/grocery-margins-2025]
- Customer satisfaction correlates with price at r=0.72 (p<0.01) [source: https://example.com/consumer-satisfaction-meta-analysis]

## Sources
| Source | URL | Accessed |
|--------|-----|----------|
| Retail Industry Benchmarks 2025 | https://example.com/retail-benchmarks-2025 | 2025-01-25 |
| Regional Sales Performance Report | https://example.com/regional-sales-report | 2025-01-25 |
| Pricing Elasticity Study | https://example.com/pricing-elasticity-study | 2025-01-25 |
| Grocery Margin Analysis 2025 | https://example.com/grocery-margins-2025 | 2025-01-25 |
| Consumer Satisfaction Meta-Analysis | https://example.com/consumer-satisfaction-meta-analysis | 2025-01-25 |

## Confidence
High -- 5 independent external sources confirm key findings across revenue distribution, pricing, and satisfaction metrics.

## Dead Ends
- Attempted seasonal trend analysis but 10-day window insufficient for meaningful patterns.

## Suggested Follow-ups
- Extend dataset to 90+ days for seasonal analysis
- Cross-category discount optimization modeling
